calendar.matters:
ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING, FEES
Off Calendar Mar-01-2011 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Lillian Ng for Michelle Booker. Albert Kun for Denise Booker (Objector).

Counsel presented oral argument to support their positions with regard to the efforts/lack of efforts made to collect on the note. The Court overruled the objection and approved the Final Report and Account (subject to review of the order).

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Reporter: Diana Chen, CSR#11312. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy Clauzel.

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING, FEES
Off Calendar Feb-15-2011 Continued To Mar-01-11 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Albert Kun for and with Denise Booker. Lillian Ng for Michelle Booker.

Mr. Kun requested two weeks to file his objections. Ms. Ng. objected to a continuance. The matter was continued to March 1, 2011 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 to allow Mr. Kun time to review the records.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Reporter: Diana Chen, CSR#11312. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy A. Payne.

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING, FEES
Off Calendar Jan-04-2011 Continued To Feb-15-11 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Lillian Ng for and with Michelle Booker and Patrice Booker (Trustees). Denise Booker in Propria Persona.

Denise Booker requested time to obtain Counsel, stated that she did not receive copies of all of the financial records and questioned what happened to her deceased Sister's distribution. Ms. Ng provided case history, stated that they had not been successful in collecting the indebtedness owed to the Estate, that all records had been provided to all Parties, that all Beneficiaries except for two had signed consents and requested the matter be brought to an end. The Court expressed concern about the amount of time this matter has been pending. The matter was continued to February 15, 2011 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 to allow Denise Booker to obtain Counsel and file her objection if desired. Ms. Ng will file the original bank statements.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Reporter: Diana Chen, CSR#11312. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy A. Payne.
